CheckTitle: System-preferred multifactor authentication is enabled for all users
Description: The tenant authentication methods policy should have **system-preferred multifactor authentication** (**systemCredentialPreferences**) enabled and targeting **all users**. System-preferred MFA prompts users to authenticate with the most secure method they have registered.
Risk: Without **system-preferred** **MFA**, users may default to weaker registered methods (such as SMS) instead of stronger ones (such as Authenticator push or passwordless), lowering the overall assurance of multifactor authentication.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center at https://entra.microsoft.com/
2. Go to **Protection** > **Authentication methods** > **Settings**
3. Set **System-preferred multifactor authentication** to **Enabled** and Target **All users**
4. Click **Save**

Recommendation.Text: Enable system-preferred multifactor authentication for all users so Entra prompts each user to sign in with the most secure method they have registered.
